WARNING: These test points are for A2338_M2 (820-02773) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files.
title: 5V DCPS Triage — No Power / Dead Board
description: Step-by-step diagnostic for A2338_M2 MacBook Pro 13" presenting with no power. Uses 5V DCPS (DC Power Supply) on USB-C port. Board has NO MagSafe — USB-C only.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws almost nothing at 5V. CD3217 not passing VBUS, charger not loading, or PPBUS_AON open.

steps.measure: Probe 5V on PPVBUS_USBC0 at UF400 (CD3217 ATC0)
steps.if_missing: USB-C connector damage, open trace, or CD3217 (UF400) MOSFET failure. Checks: Inspect USB-C connector for bent/broken pins; Check continuity from USB-C VBUS pins to CF400; Check CC lines (USBC0_CC1, USBC0_CC2) for shorts to ground

steps.measure: Probe Should rise toward battery voltage (~4-5V from DCPS at 5V, or 12.6V if battery connected) at U5200 (Charger IC, XMC260 inductor)
steps.expected: Should rise toward battery voltage (~4-5V from DCPS at 5V, or 12.6V if battery connected)
steps.if_missing: Charger IC U5200 fault, or input not reaching charger. Checks: Check SYS_DETECT at TP5100/TP5101 (page 24) — should be high when battery flex connected; Check CHGR_AUX_OK signal (page 26) — should be pulled up; Check for short on PPBUS_AON (diode mode on PBUS caps)

scenarios.meaning: Some current draw but not enough for full startup. PP3V8_AON or PMU outputs may be missing.

steps.measure: Probe 3.8V at SVR AON controller (30A ICC MAX, pages 28-29)
steps.component: SVR AON controller (30A ICC MAX, pages 28-29)
steps.if_missing: SVR AON controller failure, enable not asserted, or downstream short pulling rail down. Checks: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN — is the enable asserted? (U5349 delay circuit, page 26); Check PPVAON_FAULT_L — is fault signal active low? (page 28); Diode mode check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN caps — common short target

steps.measure: Probe {'PP1V8_AON_MPMU': '1.8V (MPMU LDO 9)', 'PP1V2_AON_MPMU': '1.2V (MPMU LDO 9B)', 'PP1V05_LDOINT_MPMU': '1.05V (MPMU LDOINT)', 'PP3V_AON': '3.0V (MPMU LDO 2)'} at U8100 (MPMU)
steps.if_missing: MPMU U8100 failure, or short on AON LDO output. Checks: Diode mode each rail for shorts; If PP1V8_AON shorted — check for short to ground on PP1V8_AON_MPMU net; If all AON LDOs missing — U8100 itself is likely dead

scenarios.meaning: Excessive current draw indicates short on main bus or downstream rail.

steps.if_missing: UC260 failure, enable not asserted, or downstream short. Checks: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN — should be asserted after PMU startup (MPMU GPIO, page 40); Check P5VS2TPS_PGOOD output (page 43); Diode mode PP5V_S2 output for short
UC710 failure, enable not asserted, or downstream short. Checks: Check P3V3S2_PWR_EN — should be asserted after PMU startup; Check P3V3S2_PGOOD output (page 45); Diode mode PP3V3_S2 output for short
